named_technique: MEGA-12: joint mod-44100 closure + general-d mod-36 + squarefree-d coprimality via p-adic v_p
seminal_paper_arxiv: Golomb 1970 (Amer. Math. Monthly 77, 848-852); Beckon 2019 (Rose-Hulman UMJ 20 no 2 paper 3)
bridge_lemma: Combine three blocks: (A) MEGA-3 mod-44100 closure via 10 new atomic lemmas for mod-25 residues 5,10,15,20 and mod-49 residues 7,14,21,28,35,42, (B) MEGA-11 generalizes to arbitrary d via three-fold mod-4 and mod-9 obstructions on n, n+d, n+2d, (C) MEGA-8 squarefree-d coprimality via D1 structural lemma iterated over Squarefree d. Intersection on squarefree d pins joint (n mod 36, d mod 36) to coprime subset of 259 admissible pairs.
informal_proof_outline: Goal: prove the three-conjunct META theorem. STRATEGY: use the slot-1325 atomic obstruction template, replicate it for p=5 and p=7, generalize to arbitrary d via three-fold application, then layer in the squarefree-d coprimality argument from MEGA-8. STEP 1 (conjunct (a), MEGA-3 mod-44100). Prove 10 new atomic obstruction lemmas: not_powerful_of_mod25_eq{5,10,15,20} and not_powerful_of_mod49_eq{7,14,21,28,35,42}. Each follows the same 3-line proof: from hpow p hp_prime hp_dvd we obtain p^2 ∣ x, but h gives x mod p^2 = r where p ∣ r and p^2 ∤ r, contradiction by omega. Then case-split on n%25 ∈ Fin 25 (25 cases) and n%49 ∈ Fin 49 (49 cases); for each non-admissible residue, identify which of {n, n+1, n+2} has the forbidden mod-p^2 residue and apply the corresponding atomic lemma. The slot-1325 mod-4 and mod-9 conclusions (n%4=3 and n%9 ∈ {0,7,8}) follow as before. STEP 2 (conjunct (b), MEGA-11 general-d). Three applications of slot-1325's not_powerful_of_mod4_eq2 to hn, hn1 (Powerful (n+d)), hn2 (Powerful (n+2*d)) give the mod-4 non-residue conclusions. Three applications of not_powerful_of_mod9_eq{3,6} give the mod-9 non-residue conclusions. STEP 3 (conjunct (c), MEGA-8 squarefree coprimality). Use the D1 structural lemma (powerful_3AP_prime_once_not_dvd already drafted in MEGA-8's Lean file): for prime p with v_p(d)=1, if Powerful n + Powerful (n+d) then ¬ (p ∣ n). Iterate over the squarefree prime factors of d: by_contra hne, get a common prime p ∣ Nat.gcd n d, apply the lemma to derive ¬ (p ∣ n), contradiction. STEP 4 (assemble). The three conjuncts combine via And.intro. Mathlib infrastructure verified resident: Nat.prime_two, prime_three, prime_five, prime_seven, omega, native_decide, Squarefree, Nat.Coprime, gcd. MEGA-8's draft chapter Lean file (submissions/yolo_mega8_powerful_chapter.lean) already contains the slot-1325-style atomic lemmas for mod 4 and mod 9 plus the D1 structural lemma and the squarefree-d corollary, so a substantial fraction of the proof is essentially copy-paste from there.
candidate_lemmas: L_mod4_eq2 (slot 1325, USE): For x in N, x % 4 = 2 → ¬ Nat.Powerful x. Proof: contradiction via hpow 2 prime_two and omega.
L_mod9_eq{3,6} (slot 1325, USE): For x in N, x % 9 ∈ {3,6} → ¬ Nat.Powerful x. Same template.

L_mod25_eq{5,10,15,20} (MEGA-3 NEW): For x in N, x % 25 ∈ {5,10,15,20} → ¬ Nat.Powerful x. Proof: from x mod 25 = r ∈ {5,10,15,20}, deduce 5 ∣ x by omega, hence 25 ∣ x by Powerful, contradicting x % 25 = r ≠ 0.
L_mod49_eq{7,14,21,28,35,42} (MEGA-3 NEW): For x in N, x % 49 ∈ {7,14,21,28,35,42} → ¬ Nat.Powerful x. Same template with p=7.

L_decompose_d1_mod44100 (MEGA-3 NEW): For n in N, Powerful n + Powerful (n+1) + Powerful (n+2) → the conjunction (n%4=3) ∧ (n%9 ∈ {0,7,8}) ∧ (n%25 ∈ T25) ∧ (n%49 ∈ T49). Proof: omega case-splits + the atomic obstructions.

L_decompose_gen_d_mod36 (MEGA-11 NEW): For n d in N with 0<d, Powerful n + Powerful (n+d) + Powerful (n+2*d) → mod-4 and mod-9 non-residue conclusions for each of n, n+d, n+2*d. Trivial three-fold application.

L_D1_structural (MEGA-8/D1, slot 1329 sketch): For prime p, n d in N with v_p(d)=1, Powerful n + Powerful (n+d) → ¬ (p ∣ n). Proof: by_contra; hpow gives p^2 ∣ n, dvd_add gives p ∣ n+d hence p^2 ∣ n+d, subtracting gives p^2 ∣ d contradicting v_p(d)=1.

L_squarefree_d_coprime (MEGA-8 META, MEGA-12 included): For Squarefree d > 0, n d in N, Powerful n + Powerful (n+d) + Powerful (n+2*d) → Nat.Coprime n d. Proof: iterate L_D1_structural over d.primeFactors (each prime has v_p(d)=1 by Squarefree).

L_meta_mega12 (MEGA-12 ASSEMBLE): conjunction of L_decompose_d1_mod44100, L_decompose_gen_d_mod36, L_squarefree_d_coprime. Proof: And.intro. Lean proof length: 80-150 lines combining slot 1325, MEGA-8's chapter draft, and 10 new atomic lemmas.
honest_disclaimer: MEGA-12 META coordinator dossier (May 31 2026, paired_llm=mega-team-meta-coordinator). Reads .fusion.json from 8 MEGA-team siblings: MEGA-3 (E364 mod-44100, fit 0.88, submitted), MEGA-5 (multiperfect ω≤2 odd, fit 0.85), MEGA-6 (E942 Kronecker conditional, fit 0.55), MEGA-7 (E938 Pell classification + falsification, fit 0.22), MEGA-8 (Powerful Chapter, fit 0.62, draft Lean already produced), MEGA-9 (E142 Roth axiomatize Bloom-Sisask, fit 0.45), MEGA-10 (E779 Deaconescu, fit 0.50, novel target), MEGA-11 (Joint mod-36 d-AP, fit 0.94). Top-3 picked by fit × Aristotle-tractability × Mathlib-novelty × cluster-density: MEGA-11 (highest fit, decidable, Mathlib-novel), MEGA-3 (very high fit, 67% sharper than Beckon mod-36, mod-44100 Mathlib-novel), MEGA-8 (chapter unifier, draft Lean already drafted, ready for upstream PR). The MEGA-12 META target combines all three into a single conjunctive theorem: (a) MEGA-3 mod-44100 closure for d=1 triples, (b) MEGA-11 mod-36 admissibility for general-d triples, (c) MEGA-8 squarefree-d coprimality. ALL three conjuncts are Mathlib-novel and unconditional. Intersection on squarefree d gives genuine NEW mathematical content beyond any individual sibling: gcd(n,d)=1 forces n,d to live in coprime residue classes of (Z/36)^2 (and at a finer scale, (Z/44100)^2 when d=1). The fusion lane CAN: (1) deliver Aristotle a single theorem statement combining three sibling building blocks; (2) leverage MEGA-8's existing draft Lean chapter file as warm-start context; (3) target a Mathlib-PR-ready chapter file Mathlib/NumberTheory/Powerful.lean. The fusion lane CANNOT: (1) close E938 (the META is a structural-restriction layer, not a finiteness proof); (2) close E364 (only refines the residue obstruction); (3) close E942 (orthogonal limsup question handled by MEGA-6/D6). Bayesian estimates for MEGA-12 META: P(target_resolved=1 within 72h, full three-conjunct compile) ≈ 0.55 — the proof is essentially decidable case-analysis (omega + native_decide) plus the D1 structural lemma; high tractability given MEGA-8's draft. P(compiled_partial with two of three conjuncts proved) ≈ 0.25. P(compile_failed) ≈ 0.08. P(compiled_no_advance) ≈ 0.12. What META adds beyond the 8 MEGA siblings: explicit conjunction L_meta_mega12 plus an explicit Mathlib-PR sequence (PR-A MEGA-8 chapter scaffold; PR-B MEGA-3 mod-44100 add-on; PR-C MEGA-11 general-d add-on; PR-D coprimality bridge) targeting Mathlib/NumberTheory/Powerful.lean. The other MEGA siblings (5, 6, 7, 9, 10) are orthogonal to this chapter and remain as standalone submissions in their own right. Per CLAUDE.md rule 10: HAVE FAITH IN ARISTOTLE.
